item.title: Teleprompter operator made over $100K betting on Trump speeches
item.description: After detecting suspicious activity on the teleprompter operator's account, Kalshi investigated and then referred the case to federal regulators.

item.title: Deadly floods hit same parts of Texas devastated by 2025 4th of July flooding
item.description: Torrential rain triggered deadly flash flooding in Central Texas this week, prompting local leaders to urge thousands of residents to seek higher ground immediately. At least one person has died and crews have rescued at least 70 people. CBS News correspondent Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Vance says Trump administration mishandled Epstein files release
item.description: It's rare for the Trump administration to admit a mistake, but Vice President JD Vance did just that on Joe Rogan's podcast, saying they mishandled the release of files related to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Political strategists Joe Borelli and Cameron French join with analysis.

item.title: Erin Brockovich on the stakes of data center expansion
item.description: New York is now the first state to temporarily ban data center construction, paving the way for others to follow suit. Environmental advocate Erin Brockovich joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Stocks fall on AI and Iran concerns, Netflix releases Q2 earnings report
item.description: The Dow, NASDAQ and S&P fell on Thursday as concerns over the AI boom and war with Iran dragged stocks into the red. Angela Palumbo, a tech news writer at Barron's, joins CBS News to discuss the markets, Netflix's earnings report and more.

item.title: What we know about Trump's primetime address
item.description: President Trump will deliver an address on election integrity and voting systems Thursday night. Olivia Rinaldi reports on what we know so far.

item.title: Maine Democrats to face off in first Senate debate since Platner withdrawal
item.description: Maine Democrats will have their first Senate debate Thursday since Graham Platner ended his campaign last week. They are expected to discuss ICE operations following the fatal shooting of an immigrant in the state. CBS News national correspondent Lilia Luciano has more from Biddeford, Maine.

item.title: Deadly helicopter crash in Hudson River likely caused by bird strike, NTSB says
item.description: A family of five from Spain, including three children, and the pilot died in the April 2025 crash.

item.title: Bank of America CEO says U.S. could see $20 billion in economic impact from World Cup
item.description: The 2026 World Cup has been an economic boon for the host cities across the U.S., Canada and Mexico. CBS News chief foreign affairs correspondent and "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan spoke with Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan about the tournament's financial impact.

item.title: What to expect from Trump's primetime speech on election security
item.description: President Trump is expected to deliver a primetime speech on election security Thursday night. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and David Becker have the latest.

item.title: Leavitt addresses new wave of U.S. strikes on Iran: "President Trump is not going to sit by"
item.description: The U.S. on Thursday launched a new wave of strikes against Iran for the fifth day in a row. CBS News senior foreign correspondent Imtiaz Tyab reports.

item.title: U.S. launches another night of strikes as battle continues over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Bank of America CEO on World Cup ticket sales, economic boost
item.description: Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan speaks with "Face the Nation" about his company's FIFA sponsorship and the high turnout for the World Cup despite expensive ticket prices. See the full interview, Sunday.

item.title: Navy's Blue Angels won't be reprimanded after video shows low flyover
item.description: The Blue Angels said an aircraft "flew lower than standard profiles, resulting in a disturbance on the beach that affected civilian chairs and umbrellas."

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: The recall includes cases of Pillsbury "Hard Roll Dough" and "Kaiser Roll Dough" bread rolls, which are marketed to businesses.

item.title: Leavitt claims Trump's primetime address "will shock you"
item.description: President Trump will allege Chinese meddling in U.S. elections during his primetime speech on Thursday night, sources say. CBS News White House reporter Olivia Rinaldi has the latest.

item.title: Wildfires bringing terrible air quality, hazy skies to much of the Midwest and Northeast
item.description: Wildfires in Ontario, Canada, and northern Minnesota are bringing dark skies and poor air quality across large swaths of the United States. CBS News' Elijah Westbrook reports from New York City, Darius Johnson reports from Chicago and meteorologist Jessica Burch has a look at when the smoke might dissipate.

item.title: Dozens rescued as Central Texas hit by flash flooding for third day in a row
item.description: Dozens of people have been rescued as water levels rise in Central Texas amid torrential rainfall. CBS News correspondent Jason Allen reports from the flood zone.

item.title: Tillis says he won't support Blanche unless he meets with Epstein survivors
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis said that Todd Blanche must meet with survivors of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein's crimes before he'll vote to advance his nomination for attorney general out of the Senate Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Key takeaways from day two of Blanche confirmation hearing
item.description: CBS News senior justice department reporter Sarah Lynch breaks down the highlights from day two of Todd Blanche's Senate confirmation hearings for attorney general.

item.title: Watch: Karoline Leavitt answers questions on Trump's primetime speech, new Iran strikes
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt spoke to reporters Thursday about President Trump's upcoming primetime speech, the latest strikes against Iran and more.

item.title: Schiff presses Ashcroft over Trump's calls for prosecution of perceived political enemies
item.description: Sen. Adam Schiff questioned former Attorney General John Ashcroft Thursday about President Trump's calls to prosecute his perceived political enemies. Ashcroft was appearing before the Senate in support of Todd Blanche's confirmation as attorney general.

item.title: Trump Media to sell faster access to president's Truth Social posts
item.description: "Markets already move on Truth Social posts," the company's chief executive said in the Truth API announcement.

item.title: Book excerpt: "Mighty Real: A History of LGBTQ Music, 1969-2000" by Barry Walters
item.description: Barry Walters, a writer for such publications as Rolling Stone and Spin, explores how LGBTQ songwriters, musicians, execs and fans reshaped pop culture in the late 20th century, as queer messages in music became less coded.

item.title: Doctors question plan to screen military members for low testosterone
item.description: Doctors raised a range of concerns about a testosterone screening policy announced by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, which is likely to be costly to implement.

item.title: Airlines say permanent daylight saving time won't be simple
item.description: Adjusting to permanent daylight saving time would cause significant disruptions to schedules and operations, an airline trade group said.

item.title: Half of Young Adults Still Live at Home | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description: Half of American adults under 30 are now living with their parents. Is this the new normal, or just a temporary consequence of today's housing market? Regardless, Jill advises young adults living at home to at least offer to chip in financially. Plus, Mark explains why obsessing over minimizing taxes can lead to costly investing mistakes, and Jill shares her thoughts on how friends with different income levels should approach splitting the cost of dinners, vacations, and group outings.

item.title: Hegseth requires testosterone deficiency screening for service members over 30
item.description: The Pentagon will now require service members over 30 to screen for testosterone deficiency, and if needed, they can elect to have testosterone replacement therapy.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: In the 70mm IMAX projection booth with "Interstellar"
item.description: Director Christopher Nolan's futuristic sci-fi spectacle is receiving a rare theatrical release on old-school, large-format film stock. CBSNews.com's David Morgan enters the projection booth where the new IMAX movie is being screened in 70mm.
